rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order 1) Approving Receiver Final Account And Report, 2) Approving Receiver Fees And Expenses, 3) Approving Distribution Of Funds, 4) Discharging Receiver, 5) Exonerating Bond, 6) Requiring Court Permission And Funding The Receivership In Order To File Claim That Involves Receiver, 7) Retaining Jurisdiction Re Matters Or Claims Involving Receiver, 8) Authorizing Plaintiff To Obtain Residual Proceeds From Case Cgc 18-564941; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Kevin Whelan In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 14, 2022 line 1. RECEIVER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER 1) APPROVING RECEIVER'S FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT, 2) APPROVING RECEIVER'S FEES AND EXPENSES, ETC. is GRANTED as proposed by the receiver, except for the distribution to the Plaintiff. The Court is inclined to authorize the distribution with a deduction for a reserve fund to cover Umpqua's incurred and potential fees to litigate 4 outstanding appeals. Hearing Required to address the amount of the reserve to be held by the Receiver in Umpqua matter to cover such amounts. The Court makes no determination as to the final amount of fees or entitlement to fees at this time. This issue will be addressed upon the conclusion of the appeals and any interested parties will be entitled to submit their claims to the reserve funds. By no later than January 14, 2022 Umpqua and Plaintiff to submit supplemental declarations. Not to exceed two pages stating the amount of reasonable and necessary anticipated fees on the 4 appeals that may be incurred by Umpqua. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants And Verpresar Llc Motion For Court Order To Determine Necessity Of Appeal Bond To Stay Receiver Sale Of Property Pending Appeal Of Post-Judgment Collateral Sale Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 14, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION FOR COURT ORDER TO DETERMINE NECESSITY OF APPEAL BOND TO STAY RECEIVER SALE OF PROPERTY PENDING APPEAL OF POST-JUDGMENT COLLATERAL SALE ORDER is GRANTED in part and MOOT in part. The moving party requests a determination of necessity of appeal bond. The posting of a bond or undertaking is required in order to stay receivership proceedings pending an appeal. (Code Civ. Proc., 917.4 [undertaking required to stay sale of property in receivership]; City of Riverside v. Horspool (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 670, 672). See CCSF v. Kihagi A162609 November 2, 2021 order. To the extent the motion seeks the determination of the bond amount, the motion is moot in light of January 4, 2022 sale. After the property has been sold there is no relief a trial court can grant. (City of Riverside at 682). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 14, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT ERIC KETCHUM'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 14, 2022 line 7.
